big_picture: The Rajya Sabha passed the Central Armed Police Force (General Administration) Bill, 2026, through a voice vote amidst an Opposition walkout. The Opposition wanted the Bill to go to the Select Committee. The Lok Sabha passed a Bill to declare Amaravati as the capital of Andhra Pradesh. The government hiked commercial LPG, premium fuel prices, but kept domestic LPG, regular petrol, and diesel prices unchanged. Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government cut special additional excise duty on petrol by Rs 10 per litre to shield consumers from rising crude prices. Several migrant workers from West Bengal reported instances of deletions or discrepancies in the electoral rolls following the revision exercise, prompting them to rush home amid voter list fears ahead of West Bengal polls. The Supreme Court quashed a dowry harassment case against a woman's parents-in-law and sister-in-law, emphasizing that unexplained delay in initiating criminal proceedings can be fatal in matrimonial disputes.

Legal.summary:
The Supreme Court granted an interim stay on the trial proceedings against Tamil Nadu Minister Durai Murugan in a disproportionate assets case. 

The Supreme Court urged the Delhi High Court Chief Justice to consider a policy decision for expeditious trials in sensitive cases, while dealing with a PIL on racial discrimination against Northeastern people. 

The Supreme Court observed that it had no reason to doubt the Madhya Pradesh High Court's decision to consider objections recorded during an ASI survey videography in the Bhojshala Temple-Kamal Maula Mosque dispute.

Politics.summary:
PM Modi has stated that the Congress party is conspiring to protect infiltrators in Assam, as he campaigns for the BJP to win a third consecutive term in the state. 

Mamata Banerjee has warned TMC leaders that new Election Commission officers may reject their nominations in the upcoming West Bengal elections, and accused the BJP of misusing agencies and seeking voters' bank details. 

BJP MP Nishikant Dubey has apologized unconditionally for his remarks linking former Odisha CM Biju Patnaik to Nehru and the CIA in the 1960s.
Politics.top_article_title: PM Modi Bats For BJP Hat-Trick In Assam

Agriculture.summary:
A panel has advised the government to take steps to protect farmers from cheap edible oil imports. This could potentially benefit farmers who are struggling with low prices for their produce.

The government of India has stated that there are adequate fertilizer stocks ahead of the Kharif season, but the cost of importing liquefied natural gas (LNG) from the spot market could increase the burden of fertilizer subsidies.

India is seeking new sources to fill the fertilizer gap ahead of the Kharif season, as the country currently has a shortage of 21 million tonnes of fertilizer, leaving a deficit of 21 million tonnes.
Agriculture.top_article_title: Panel: Take steps to protect farmers against cheap edible oil imports

Defence.summary:
The Indian Air Force is set to receive two mountain radars worth ₹1,950 crore as part of a deal with Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) to enhance its capabilities and boost national security. 

A devastating incident in Iran on February 28 has raised questions about the application of International Humanitarian Law during the intensifying conflict in the country. 

Shares of Garden Reach Shipbuilders Engineers have increased by 20% after the company reported a record turnover of ₹6,400 crore in the fiscal year 2026, a 26% year-on-year growth.
Defence.top_article_title: IAF to get mountain radars worth  ₹1,950 cr

Energy.summary:
Commercial LPG prices in Delhi have increased by Rs 195.5, making a 19-kg cylinder cost Rs 2,078.5. 
Suresh Gopi, a Union Minister, will have to face trial in a petition challenging his election. 
Urban Indian households primarily use liquefied petroleum gas for cooking, while many rural households still rely on firewood.
Energy.top_article_title: Commercial LPG prices increased by Rs 195.5 amid Iran war

Finance.summary:
The Reserve Bank of India imposed a monetary penalty of 31.80 lakh Rupees on Airtel Payments Bank Limited. 

The central government's fiscal deficit stood at 80.4 percent of its annual budget target till February, amounting to 12.52 lakh crore Rupees. 

Inflation target, IPO deadlines, polluted cities, and GDP growth cut are highlighted in a weekly compilation of data-based insights presented through charts.
Finance.top_article_title: Reserve Bank of India imposes monetary penalty on Airtel Payments Bank Limited

Governance.summary:
Odisha Governor Hari Babu Kambhampati announced the start of self-enumeration for the Census 2027 in Odisha. 

Mubarik Gul of the National Conference foresaw the abrogation of Article 370, stating it would be removed and the state would be split. 

Jammu and Kashmir backed a private member land Bill, but Sajjad Lone expressed concerns about bias towards the "super elite".

Foreign.summary:
Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov is scheduled to visit Delhi in mid-May for a BRICS meeting. 

Canadian Foreign Minister Anita Anand discussed trade and West Asia with Indian Foreign Minister Jaishankar on the sidelines of a G7 meeting. 

Two routes, including a backup, and a sanctions hack are used to transport Russian oil to India, which travels approximately 7,500 nautical miles from Baltic ports to Gujarat shores.

Infrastructure.summary:
A Parliamentary Panel has flagged aviation safety concerns and recommended an audit of Air India since the Tata Group takeover. The panel suggested that the Ministry of Civil Aviation should ask the Comptroller and Auditor General to conduct the audit.

The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has raised Rs 28,307 crore through monetisation in 2025-26, and is hopeful of achieving the Rs 30,000 crore target for the fiscal year 2025-26. 

The Railways has upgraded the 161-year-old Yamuna Bridge in Uttar Pradesh, which is expected to improve train services on key routes.
Infrastructure.top_article_title: Parliamentary Panel flags aviation safety concerns, seeks Air India audit since Tata takeover

Trade.summary:
The WTO ministerial in Cameroon ended in deadlock, failing to extend the e-commerce moratorium due to deep divisions between major powers. On the sidelines of the WTO Ministerial, India and the US discussed next steps in BTA talks.

India has scrapped the ₹10 lakh cap on courier exports and simplified returns, aiming to boost its online exporters by cutting costs and delays.
Trade.top_article_title: Away from Middle East war zone, many battles unfold on the table

Labour.summary:
The Bombay High Court ruled that employees are not liable for their employer's failure to maintain Provident Fund records, directing the EPFO to reconsider higher pension claims fairly. 

In India, workers are going on strike today to protest against four new labour codes, with the economic fallout from the US-Israel war on Iran contributing to their struggles. 

The Telangana government passed a landmark Bill that allows for docking the salary of employees who neglect their parents.

Technology.summary:
The Finance Ministry has cleared over ₹1 lakh crore for India Semiconductor Mission 2.0, which will focus on research and development, design, and advanced nodes, with Cabinet approval expected soon. 

Google has teased a screenless fitness wearable, while Whoop has reached a $10 billion valuation. 

Douglas Kramer, chief legal officer at Cloudflare, discussed overblocking, piracy orders, and AI crawlers in a full interview.

The state of Maharashtra in India is using local priests, known as 'pujaris', to help combat malaria in the Gadchiroli region. 

India has flagged concerns over the misuse of GLP-1 weight loss and diabetes drugs, outlining rules to regulate their use and highlighting potential serious side effects.

Anthropic's AI tool Claude Code had its source code accidentally exposed due to a packaging error, but no user data or core AI systems were affected.

articles.title: Earning Mother Need Not Be Impleaded In Child Maintenance Claim Against Father But Court Must Assess Joint Income: Allahabad HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court has observed that an earning mother need not be formally impleaded as a party in a maintenance plea filed by a child against her/his father. A bench of Justice Madan Pal Singh , however, directed that in such cases, the trial court must consider the financial capacity of both earning parents while determining the final maintenance amount based on the principle of shared parental responsibility. The single judge passed this order while hearing a criminal revision petition

Inflationary pressures may increase, impacting consumer sentiment.

articles.summary: FY26 India IPOs show signs of retail fatigue, with average listing gains falling to -7% and retail oversubscription halving. FY27 sees a robust pipeline, with 144 SEBI-approved companies and 83 new-age tech firms preparing to raise capital.

articles.title: No Law Bars Conducting Prayer Meeting Inside One's Home, Permission From Authority Not Required: Chhattisgarh High Court
articles.summary: The Chhattisgarh High Court recently observed that there is no law restraining any one from organizing prayer meeting in their dwelling house and thus no prior permission is needed from any authority for conducting such a prayer meeting if it is organized without violating any law.� In doing so the court granted relief to two persons who had moved the high court challenging notices issued by the Police restraining them from oragnizing prayer meetings for Christians inside their own dwelling home

articles.title: Kill The Notification, Not The Animal
articles.summary: Ishita Sharma Abstract: India’s Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972 contains two pathways to lethal authorisation: Section 11, which requires case-by-case justification tied to a specific dangerous animal, and Section 62, which allows the Central Government to declare an entire species ‘vermin,’ stripping statutory protection at scale without evidence, expert review, or mandatory recording of outcomes. articles.title: Away from Middle East war zone, many battles unfold on the table
articles.summary: The WTO ministerial in Cameroon ended in deadlock, failing to extend the e-commerce moratorium and highlighting deep divisions between major powers and emerging economies. Disagreements over digital taxation, intellectual property, and rule-making reveal a fractured global economic order, with Brazil and India pushing back against US and China-backed initiatives.

articles.title: NHAI raises Rs 28,307 crore through monetisation in 2025-26; hopeful of achieving the Rs 30,000 crore target for FY’26
articles.summary: The ministry of road transport and highways, on Monday, said the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) monetised over 310 km of National Highways under InvIT Round-5 for Rs 6366.98 crore while realising Rs 3,087 crore from the toll-operate-transfer (TOT) Bundle-18. With bids received for TOT Bundle-19, which are under technical evaluation, NHAI is well poised to achieve the government’s budgeted target of Rs 30,000 crore for current FY 2025–26, it said.
articles.raw_summary: The ministry of road transport and highways, on Monday, said the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) monetised over 310 km of National Highways under InvIT Round-5 for Rs 6366.98 crore while realising Rs 3,087 crore from the toll-operate-transfer (TOT) Bundle-18. With bids received for TOT Bundle-19, which are under technical evaluation, NHAI is well poised to achieve the government’s budgeted target of Rs 30,000 crore for current FY 2025–26, it said.

articles.title: Supervisory Data Quality Index for Scheduled Commercial Banks  (December 2025)
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released Supervisory Data Quality Index (sDQI) for SCBs for December 2025 . It measures data quality in terms of the Accuracy, Timeliness, Completeness and Consistency in the submission of returns. The objective of sDQI is to assess the adherence to the principles enunciated in the Master Direction on Filing of Supervisory Returns 2024 . (Brij Raj)             Chief General Manager Press Release: 2025-2026/2361

A bench of Justice Madan Pal Singh thus reduced the monthly maintenance awarded to a wife under Section 125 CrPC, taking into account the wife's professional qualifications and past employment as a radiologist The single judge modified a Family Court order, lowering the maintenance amount from Rs 18,000 to Rs 12,000 per month. articles.title: CHRIST (Deemed to be University), Pune Hosts THEMIS 6th Moot Court Competition 2026
articles.summary: The School of Law, CHRIST (Deemed to be University), Pune Lavasa Campus , successfully hosted its flagship event, THEMIS – 6th Moot Court Competition, 2026, from March 27 to 29, 2026. The competition featured a prize pool of ₹3,00,000 , along with individual awards for Best Speaker, Best Researcher, and Best Memorial. The 2026 moot proposition focused on contemporary issues in constitutional law, federalism, and data governance, encouraging participants to engage with evolving legal challenges.

articles.title: Call For papers; Delhi Journal For Law And Justice Invites Submissions For 2026 Issue
articles.summary: The Delhi Journal for Law and Justice (DJLJ) is the student-run journal of Law Centre-II, Faculty of Law, University of Delhi . It is a double-blind peer-reviewed academic journal focused on publishing original, high-quality research in the field of law and justice. The journal encourages discussion on important legal issues and promotes critical engagement with legal theories and practices. It contributes to the ongoing discussion on justice, governance, rights, and duties in India and beyond.

articles.title: Writ Petition Not Maintainable Before Final Order In Court Martial Process: Delhi High Court
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has held that a writ petition challenging disciplinary and court martial proceedings is not maintainable before the passing of a final order. A Division Bench of Justices V. Kameswar Rao and Manmeet Pritam Singh Arora observed, “...it is a settled position of law that till such time a final order is passed in the proceedings of this nature, any interference without waiting for the final decision on the proceedings by the High Court in a writ petition is uncalled for.” Relian

articles.title: Dedicated rare earth corridors will reduce import dependence: Govt
articles.summary: India is establishing dedicated rare earth corridors in Odisha, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Tamil Nadu to reduce import dependence and boost domestic capabilities in strategic sectors. A joint working group is formulating guidelines to implement these initiatives.

articles.title: Courier export cap removed to boost ecomm shipments, cut logistics delays
articles.summary: Starting April 1, India has abolished the ₹10 lakh threshold on courier exports, a shift aimed at propelling e-commerce forward and supporting small enterprises. articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's net short position in forwards rose to $77 billion in February. This move impacts the central bank's capacity to protect the rupee. Despite these challenges, India's economy shows strong momentum.

articles.title: Iran war shock: Middle East conflict could cut 1 ppt from India’s FY27 GDP outlook
articles.summary: Iran War: A persistent Middle East conflict could reduce India's real GDP growth by 1 percentage point and increase retail inflation by 1.5 percentage points from baseline estimates. This impact stems from disruptions to global oil and energy markets, affecting employment-intensive sectors and aggregate demand. The government may need to implement countercyclical policies and augment the Economic Stabilization Fund.

articles.title: India diesel exports jump 20% in March amid Iran war
articles.summary: India boosted diesel exports significantly in March. This surge was driven by favorable economics for middle distillates. Geopolitical events in West Asia tightened supply, increasing diesel and jet fuel prices. Refiners adjusted production to capitalize on these higher margins. India also increased domestic LPG production to compensate for lost imports. Overall refined product exports saw a slight decline.

articles.title: Pulses import duties likely to stay unchanged
articles.summary: New Delhi is set to maintain current import duties on pulses. The government will also extend the duty-free period for tur and urad. This move aims to prevent price increases. articles.title: India's net international liability comes down by $10.9 billion in the third quarter
articles.summary: India's financial obligations to the world decreased by $10.9 billion in the third fiscal quarter. This improvement was driven by Indian residents increasing their overseas assets more than foreigners increased their assets in India. The ratio of India's international assets to liabilities saw a positive shift. Indian residents invested more abroad in direct investments and deposits.

The trend indicates a continued expansion in industrial undertakings.

articles.summary: The government is set to review its electronics manufacturing scheme. Beneficiaries must invest in product design and achieve Six Sigma standards. This move aims to boost high-quality, self-reliant electronics manufacturing in India.

A shift towards logistics and supply chain activities is also evident.
